visual enhancers.surfaces: Grid Borders (The 'Technical' Look). Use sharp edges with a maximum border-radius of 4px (rounded-sm). Use flat solid surfaces. Absolutely NO GLASSMORPHISM to prevent visual refraction of scientific data.
visual enhancers.shadows: Minimal. Rely on 1px borders instead of shadows to define hierarchy. On hover, use a very subtle drop shadow or an inner border glow.
visual enhancers.borders: 1px solid #414868. Use border-collapse for metrics to create an IDE-like data grid.

grids.dashboard: Bento Grid Protocol (Mode B: High Density). grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4. Tight gaps (gap-4). Every pixel utilized.
grids.task_screen: Centralized focused layout. max-w-4xl mx-auto, high negative space around the central stimulus grid.
grids.n_back_grid: Dynamic grid-cols-3 to grid-cols-9 based on settings. Perfect squares with 1px borders.

layouts.home_dashboard: Sidebar navigation (icons + text). Top header with current streak and quick stats. Main content area packed with recent sessions, PRs, and weekly activity charts.
layouts.settings_form: Dense, 2-column layout for desktop. VSCode settings style. Label on left, control on right, separated by subtle borders.
layouts.task_active: Immersive fullscreen-like view. Hide sidebar. Stimuli center stage. Progress/Stats minimized to top/bottom edges.
layouts.analytics: Massive grid of charts. Line charts spanning full width, radar/pie taking half width.

motion.transitions: Fast, precise, IDE-like. 150ms ease-in-out. DO NOT use 'transition-all'. Target specific properties.
motion.interactions: Buttons and active states get a subtle scaling (-translate-y-[1px]) or an inset border highlight.
motion.task_stimuli: Zero animation on stimulus appearance to ensure exact scientific timing and reaction time measurement. Instant toggle.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure APCA standards. All text over dark backgrounds must be legible.
accessibility.testing: All interactive elements MUST have a data-testid attribute formatted as kebab-case and role-based (e.g., data-testid='start-session-button').
accessibility.motion: Respect prefers-reduced-motion. Add aria-hidden='true' to purely decorative elements.

media.icons: lucide-react. Stroke width 1.5 to match the technical, precise aesthetic.
media.empty_states: Use huge, low-opacity (opacity-10) lucide-react icons instead of illustrations to maintain the professional tool vibe.

image_urls.description: Dark geometric pattern to be used as a very low opacity (e.g., opacity-5) background texture behind hero or empty state sections to prevent flat black monotony.

components_instructions.button: Sharp corners (rounded-sm). Primary uses #7aa2f7 background with #1a1b26 text. Outline uses border with transparent background and explicitly defined text color on hover.
components_instructions.input: Flat, #1f2335 background. 1px border. On focus, border changes to primary color. High density.
components_instructions.card: Background #24283b, 1px solid border #414868. No default shadow.
components_instructions.charts: Use Recharts. LineChart, BarChart, ScatterChart, RadarChart, Heatmap. Tooltips must have dark backgrounds (#24283b) and match IDE theme.
components_instructions.tables: Dense rows. Hover states light up the row slightly (#292e42). JetBrains Mono for all numeric columns.

instructions_to_main_agent: Implement a pure offline, browser-storage-only architecture. Ensure all React files use the .js extension. Adopt the Tokyo Night dark IDE aesthetic strictly. Avoid clutter and generic designs.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use solid backgrounds. DO NOT USE glass-morphism effects for this scientific application. This overrides any generic glassmorphism rules.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add `.App { text-align: center; }` in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style. (Use sharp, rounded-sm for this IDE theme).
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable for layouts, but keep data tables and forms dense.
Subtle grain textures, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Use `sonner` for toasts. Sonner component are located in `/app/src/components/ui/sonner.js`.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute to facilitate robust automated testing.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
DO NOT use a completely transparent background on headers or overlays over content.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN. Use lucide-react icons instead of emojis.
